text:
Matt Baker has shared a harrowing recollection of when he felt his family "in trouble" following an alarming episode on their farm.

Back in 2021, an accident saw Matt's 77-year-old mum Janice suffer a broken leg after being knocked down by sheep. At that point, away from home and engaged in setting up his own television production company post-departure from The One Show, the 46-year-old was envisaging a reduced on-screen presence to focus on off-screen production roles.

However, his plans took a relentless twist following an idea pitched by a TV commissioner. Amidst collating ideas for his new project, a disconcerting call from his dad broke the news of the unfortunate incident involving his mother and their immediate need for hospitalisation.

Without hesitation, the host recounted how he rapidly mobilised his family and livestock, making an urgent beeline back to their northern homestead.

He said: "My mum is the shepherdess, I mean there's no doubt about it, she runs the show up there and I knew straight away that we were going to be in trouble. We kind of, as a family, had been waiting for something like this to happen, because it was only ever going to be some farming thing or the sheep that were ever going to tell mum that things needed to change," reportsthe Express.

"Because I knew the workload that was up there, I felt I had no choice and thought let's just get back up there.

"We put all the animals in the trailer and headed up north. I just started to do these changes because we needed to change the sheep that we had."

While discussing his farmhouse disorder with television commissioners for new idea suggestions, one of them proposed bringing Matt's family farm mayhem to television viewers.

Initial hesitation was the response from the presenter who had not previously involved his family on-screen, but they warmed to the idea once he assured them he would be in charge of the production process.

With their consent, Matt went forward with filming a pilot, which led to the creation of 'Our Farm in the Dales'.

"They were actually a big part of the edit process because I was doing it all at home in lockdown," Matt revealed during an interview onBBCRadio 4's Saturday Live.

"It's unbelievable that all this happened during lockdown."

'Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales' allowed the audience to become acquainted with Matt's family, which includes his spouse Nicola and their children Molly and Luke.

Following this, the farmer took on hosting duties for 'Our Dream Farm with Matt Baker' on Channel 4, where seven pairs vied for the chance to run a 340-acre farm at the Wallington Estate.

After the series concluded its eight-episode run, a livestock auctioneer and his wife emerged as winners and started their decade-long tenancy.

Presently, in addition to his presenting roles, the former gymnast is a commentator for the Paris Olympics this year.

title: Olympics' Matt Baker knew family were 'in trouble' after worrying phone call from dad - Manchester Evening News
author: Daniel Windham
description: Former The One Show presenter Matt Baker knew his family were 'in trouble' after he was tipped off about a terrifying incident on the farm
